2606.06285.title: TRACE: A Temporal Conditional Estimation for Multimodal Time Series Foundation Models
2606.06285.paper_summary: Time series foundation models (TS-FMs) aim to learn generalizable temporal representations that can be adapted to a wide range of downstream tasks. In real-world multimodal settings, time series are frequently affected by temporal misalignment and partial modality missingness, where different modalities are observed at heterogeneous time scales or are partially absent. Existing approaches typically rely on naive imputation or masking strategies, which fail to account for cross-modal dependencies and often lead to misaligned or degraded representations. We propose TRACE, a conditional estimation paradigm for multimodal time series foundation model pipelines under missingness and irregular sampling, allowing incomplete target modalities to be systematically inferred from available auxiliary modalities. We evaluate TRACE on diverse multimodal benchmarks spanning healthcare and affective computing, including the MIMIC-IV clinical dataset and the CMU-MOSI and CMU-MOSEI benchmarks for multimodal sentiment analysis. Across a range of downstream prediction tasks and missing-modality settings, TRACE consistently outperforms prior multimodal fusion approaches, demonstrating improved robustness to severe modality missingness and more reliable cross-modal representations.
2606.06285.paper_summary_zh: 時間序列基礎模型（TS-FMs）旨在學習可泛化的時間表示，這些表示可以適應各種下游任務。在現實世界的多模態環境中，時間序列經常受到時間錯位和部分模態缺失的影響，其中不同的模態在異質的時間尺度上被觀察到或部分缺失。現有的方法通常依賴於簡單的插補或遮蔽策略，這些策略未能考慮跨模態的依賴性，並且經常導致錯位或退化的表示。我們提出了TRACE，一種在缺失和不規則取樣情況下的多模態時間序列基礎模型管道的條件估計範式，允許從可用的輔助模態系統性地推斷不完整的目標模態。我們在涵蓋醫療保健和情感計算的多樣化多模態基準上評估TRACE，包括MIMIC-IV臨床數據集以及CMU-MOSI和CMU-MOSEI的多模態情感分析基準。在各種下游預測任務和缺失模態設置中，TRACE始終優於先前的多模態融合方法，顯示出對嚴重模態缺失的改進穩健性和更可靠的跨模態表示。
2606.06285.author: Ziwen Kan et.al.
2606.06285.authors: Ziwen Kan, Yishuo Chen, Kecheng Li, Andrew Wen, Xiaomeng Wang, Liwei Wang, Jihao Duan, Song Wang, Hongfang Liu, Tianlong Chen
